title: Stream output without trusting partials
description: Streaming responses are accumulated and only validated/acted on at well-defined boundaries, never on incomplete fragments.
body: Use when consuming a streamed completion for display or parsing. Do not parse or act on a partial JSON fragment, and avoid treating a mid-stream stop as a complete answer. Actions: accumulate chunks, render display text progressively but defer structured parsing until the stream signals completion, and detect truncation (length finish-reason) explicitly. Validation signals include a test that a truncated stream is flagged rather than parsed and that structured parsing runs only on the final buffer. Risk boundary: acting on a truncated structured payload can corrupt downstream state; treat truncation as a failure, not a partial success.
